lines.text: Invasive species case study. Research, then present it — creatively, not just a poster with clip art dying on it.
lines.action: taps the whiteboard

lines.text: Groups are on the board. Two weeks. Late is a conversation, not never — but let's not test that.
Oh, here we go.
lines.action: already scanning the list before he's even out of his seat

lines.text: Find your name, Wren. Everyone else already found theirs.
...Castillo. Delgado. Nakamura. Oduya.
lines.emotion: flat, reading it off the board

lines.text: Hey, look at that. It's the whole crew.
lines.action: half-wave in Wren's direction, easy grin

lines.text: Wait, we're a group? Okay, that's — actually kind of great, I already have ideas.
lines.action: already pulling her chair toward the table before anyone agrees to anything
says nothing, just slides his sketchbook two inches closer to himself, watching

lines.text: Two weeks. In one room. With people I've known for exactly nine days.
Okay so — I'll present, obviously, since I'm not, like, scared of talking. Jordan, you're vibes. Keep it from being boring.
lines.action: already tapping notes into her phone

lines.text: Vibes. Got it. That's a real job now.
lines.action: grinning, not actually mad

lines.text: Alex, you can draw something. And Wren, you're notes — you seem like a notes person.
lines.action: doesn't quite look up when she says it

lines.text: A notes person.
Notes person!
lines.action: repeating it back, testing it, deciding it's a compliment
taps his pen against his knee, doesn't argue, doesn't agree either

lines.text: That was eleven seconds. I counted. Nobody asked what I'm actually good at.
Okay, so — hear me out. What if instead of a poster, we do it like a comic. No words, just panels. The species shows up, spreads, and you watch the ecosystem change frame by frame.
lines.action: talking fast, already sketching, forgetting to be quiet about it

lines.text: Okay, wait, that's actually kind of sick.
lines.action: leaning over to look

lines.text: It's — I don't know, it's probably dumb, I just started drawing it and—
lines.action: catching himself, shrinking a little

lines.text: That's cute, but this is a grade, not art class.
lines.action: laughs once, not quite unkindly, already scrolling past it on her phone
goes very still, closes the sketchbook halfway, says nothing

lines.text: There it is. The door closing again. That fast.
I mean — it's not not a grade thing. Ms. Patel literally said 'creatively.' This is the most creative idea anyone's had.
I'm not saying it's bad, I'm saying—
lines.action: goes still for half a second, recalibrating

lines.text: ...fine. Whatever. We can look at it.
Democracy. Beautiful.
lines.action: leaning back, pleased
glances at Wren, quick, unreadable, then back down to his notebook — keeps drawing

lines.text: That was nothing. A sentence. Why does it feel like it mattered.
Eh, whatever's easiest, right?
lines.action: shrugging, trying to smooth it over

lines.text: Whatever's easiest.
lines.action: voice flat, sliding the notebook the rest of the way shut

lines.text: Great. Poster it is. See, that was easy.
lines.action: already moving on

lines.text: He didn't argue. That's worse than if he had.
What if it's not all one or the other — one comic panel up front, like a hook, then regular poster for the rest of it.
lines.emotion: casual, floating it like it's no big deal

lines.text: ...Fine. Whatever gets it done.
lines.action: shrugging, already typing again
the smallest real smile crosses his face before he catches it

lines.text: See, this is why we needed a notes person.
lines.action: grinning at Wren

lines.text: Small thing. Costs nothing. Feels like it counted anyway.
Okay, so, my sister did literally this exact project last year. Different teacher, same rubric basically.
lines.action: spinning her laptop around

lines.text: We swap some words, change the species, done by nine. Everybody sleeps. Everybody wins.
...huh.
lines.action: chewing his pen cap, not saying yes, not saying no

lines.text: Also — I'll just tell Ms. Patel the panel idea was mine. Sounds better coming from the presenter anyway.
lines.action: says it like an afterthought, not even looking up
goes very still, doesn't look up from his sketchbook

lines.text: She said that like it costs nothing. Like it's not actually his.
Must be nice, being able to just redo everything. Some of us don't have unlimited time to be perfect.
lines.action: sharper than she means to be, arms crossed

lines.text: I'm not saying it's perfect. I'm saying it's ours.
...whatever. It was actually kind of a good idea. The panel thing.
lines.action: quieter, almost slipping

lines.text: Don't tell him I said that.
Said what?
lines.action: already grinning, pen cap in his teeth
watching this whole exchange very carefully, saying nothing

lines.text: This looks good. Genuinely. Whose idea was the comic-panel opener?
lines.action: asked lightly, glancing between them
looks at Wren, then at Alex, and waits — for once, not filling the silence herself

lines.text: Go on. Tell her.
lines.action: nudging Alex with his elbow, low voice

lines.text: This looks good. Whose idea was the comic-panel opener?
lines.action: asked lightly
arms still crossed, looking anywhere but at Wren
goes very still, waiting to see what happens, half-expecting nothing

lines.text: This structure looks really familiar, actually...
lines.action: said lightly, already moving on to the next table
goes pale, kicks Wren's foot under the table without looking at her

lines.text: Okay, honestly? That was not a normal teacher sentence.
lines.action: whispering, eyes wide
closes his notebook slowly, watching Wren

lines.text: She's not going to say it again. She doesn't have to. That's the whole point.
You told her? Before you said literally one word to me?
lines.action: blindsided, furious, voice cracking slightly on 'me'
hovering by the lockers, wishing very hard to be somewhere else

lines.text: I wasn't trying to go around you—
That's exactly what going around someone looks like, Wren.
lines.action: her voice goes flat, controlled, worse than yelling

lines.text: Okay, honestly, everybody take a breath, this is—
lines.action: hands up, trying to map the room and failing

lines.text: I don't need you to fix it, Jordan.
lines.action: cutting him off, still looking at Wren

lines.text: Can you walk me through where this specific data point came from? It doesn't match this year's assigned species at all.
lines.action: still, unhurried, the room going quiet around her
frozen, note cards trembling slightly in her hand
eyes flicking to Wren, who's holding the note cards
goes very quiet, chewing his pen cap so hard it nearly splits

lines.text: It's not from this year's species. We used an old project as a base — that's on me, I should've said something sooner.
Thank you for telling me directly. That matters. Sit down — we'll talk after school about what happens next.
lines.action: no theatrics, just a nod
catches Wren's eye across the room afterward — something like respect in it, brief and real
doesn't add anything, doesn't throw Wren under either — just looks at the floor

lines.text: It was — mostly Mia's research, honestly, I just did notes.
lines.emotion: the words out before she can stop them

lines.action: her face does something complicated — betrayal, then a wall going up

lines.text: We'll be redoing this one. Everyone stays after to talk about it.

lines.action: doesn't say anything the rest of the day — somehow louder than if he had

lines.text: I need to tell you something before we present. The structure — it's borrowed. From last year, from Mia's sister. We're fixing it tonight, but I didn't want you to find out mid-presentation.
I appreciate you telling me now instead of later. There's a late penalty. You'll get a redo window.
lines.action: stern, but not cold

lines.text: You couldn't have told ME first? In the hallway, not, like, straight to the teacher?
lines.action: furious, arms crossed, in the hallway after

lines.text: You're right. I should've. I panicked.
...whatever. At least you didn't let me walk in there and get caught.
lines.action: grudging, not looking at her
doesn't say anything at lunch, just sits a little closer than usual

lines.text: The comic panel — that whole idea — was Alex's. Just so that's said out loud.
lines.action: clear, unhurried
startled, then quietly pleased in a way he clearly wasn't ready to feel here

lines.text: ...it did turn out better this way. Don't let it go to your head.
lines.action: grudging, but she means it

lines.text: It was kind of a group thing, honestly.
lines.emotion: easy way out, and she knows it as she says it

lines.action: doesn't seem upset exactly, just packs up a little faster than usual

lines.text: Comfortable. Not complete. I know the difference. I just didn't choose it.
I want to explain why I went to Ms. Patel. It wasn't about going around you. I was scared we'd get caught mid-presentation and it'd be worse.

lines.action: arms crossed for a long moment, looking away

lines.text: ...I just didn't think the real version was good enough. That's why I wanted the shortcut. Not because I'm lazy.
lines.action: quiet, almost slipping

lines.text: It was always good enough. It just needed more time.
Okay, honestly? This is the most real conversation this group's had in two weeks.
lines.action: trying to lighten it, but he means it

lines.text: Shut up, Jordan.
lines.emotion: but there's no heat in it — first real laugh she's cracked all week

lines.text: Alex came up with the visual concept, Mia built the whole research structure, Jordan kept us on schedule when nobody else could.
lines.action: reading it out clearly, deliberately
genuinely surprised to be thanked for something instead of just performing being thanked

lines.text: Group chat's not gonna be about damage control tonight. Historic.
lines.action: grinning, but it lands soft

lines.text: Thanks for... handling it. Earlier.
lines.action: stiff, not quite meeting Wren's eyes

lines.text: Yeah. Of course.
Progress, I guess?
lines.action: shrugging, half-joking, watching the two of them

lines.text: It's fine. It's handled. We're presenting.
lines.action: polite in the specific way that means nothing's forgiven

lines.text: See? Nobody's yelling. That's basically a win.
lines.action: trying to sound breezy, doesn't quite land
watches the two of them carefully, recalculating who's safe to trust here

lines.text: I'm sorry. Not the strategic version. I should've just talked to you instead of going to Patel first.

lines.action: doesn't fully thaw, but nods, shoulders loosening slightly

lines.text: Okay. We're good. Let's go be a group now.
lines.action: opening the classroom door, relieved

lines.text: Good job.
lines.action: said the way you'd say it to a coworker, not a friend

lines.text: You too.
It's fine. It's just not much more than fine.

options.text: Actually — maybe we should pick roles based on what people are actually into?
(Go along with it for now.)
(Say nothing to Mia — ask Alex later if he wants to team up on visuals.)

a.text: I mean — sure, if people have, like, opinions.

a.action: looks up from his sketchbook for half a second, surprised

a.text: Ooh, a democracy. Bold move, day one.

b.text: Notes. Cool. Love it here already.

b.action: already scrolling her phone, mission accomplished

c.text: (low, to Alex) Hey — want to team up on the visual stuff? Might be more fun than 'notes.'
c.action: leaning over while Mia's talking to Jordan
doesn't look up right away, but there's the smallest shift in him

options.text: That was actually a really good idea, Mia.
(Stay quiet — not your idea to defend.)
What if we do one panel as a hook, then poster for the rest?

a.text: I didn't say it was BAD—
I'm just saying — Ms. Patel said creative. This is creative.

b.action: glances at Wren, half-expecting her to say something, notices she doesn't

b.text: Not my project. Not my idea. Not my business. Right.

c.text: Okay, that's actually a good compromise, notes person.

c.action: considering it, arms still crossed but softening

options.text: No — let's actually rebuild it tonight. It's not that much more work.
(Go along with it. It's late and everyone's exhausted.)
(Say nothing now — flag it to Ms. Patel privately before class.)

a.text: It's midnight, Wren. Not everyone has, like, infinite energy for this.
I know. I'm tired too. I just don't want to turn in something that isn't ours.

b.text: ...okay. Fine. Let's just get it done.
b.emotion: the words feel wrong in her mouth even as she says them

c.action: says it easily, doesn't mean it

c.text: That's not a lie. It's just not the whole truth yet.

options.text: It'll actually be better this way — and we'll all help with your part too.
It's your call whether you're in. I'm doing it either way.

a.text: ...you'd actually help? Not just, like, say it and then not.

a.action: looking at Jordan and Alex, who both nod

b.text: ...that's kind of a jerk thing to say.
Maybe. But I'm not doing the other version.

options.text: Give Alex full credit, out loud.
Let it pass as a group effort.

a.text: That was Alex's idea. The whole panel concept — all him.

b.text: Kind of a group thing, honestly.

b.action: doesn't react outwardly, but something small closes back up

options.text: Give Alex full credit, out loud.
Let it pass as a group effort.

a.text: The comic panel was Alex's idea. I want that on record.

a.action: startled, sits up a little straighter, doesn't quite smile but almost

b.action: packs up his pencils a beat too fast when class ends

options.text: Pull Ms. Patel aside after class and tell her the truth.
Say nothing. Present as planned and hope it blows over.

a.text: Wren. Wren, what are you doing—
Fixing it before it gets worse.
a.action: already walking toward the front of the room

b.text: Okay. Okay, she was probably talking about a different group. We're fine.
b.action: talking fast, unconvincing herself as much as anyone

b.text: We are extremely not fine. But sure. Let's go with that.

options.text: Own it. Right there, in front of everyone.
Deflect — say it was mostly Mia's research.

a.text: It's not from this year's species. We used an old project as a starting point. That's on me — I should've said something before now.
Thank you for saying that clearly. Sit down. We'll talk after school.

b.text: It was — mostly Mia's research, I think.
b.emotion: the sentence tastes wrong the second it's out

b.action: her face does something complicated, then goes carefully blank

options.text: Talk to her directly — explain, and offer to fix it together.
Let Jordan smooth it over and keep some distance.

a.text: You're right that I should've talked to you first. I panicked and went to the safest option. I want to fix that — can we rebuild it together?

a.action: arms still crossed, but listening now

b.text: (to Jordan, quiet) Can you just... let her know I wasn't trying to blindside her?
I can try. I'm not, like, a translator, but I can try.
b.action: sighs, caught in the middle again

options.text: Name everyone's specific contribution out loud, including Mia's.
Keep it simple — just get through the presentation.

a.text: Alex's visual concept, Mia's research structure, Jordan's timeline wrangling. All of it real.

a.action: genuinely surprised, doesn't quite know what to do with being thanked sincerely

b.text: We all worked on it. That's really it.

b.action: stiff, formal, a truce rather than warmth

options.text: Try once more — a real apology, no strategy this time.
Let it go. Just focus on getting through the presentation cleanly.

a.text: I'm sorry. Not the version where I'm trying to fix it fast — just, I'm sorry.

a.action: doesn't fully thaw, but nods, shoulders loosening slightly

b.action: matching her tone exactly, door staying shut

lines.text: We went with the shortcut. I let it happen. Standing up now doesn't undo that — it just means I'm not adding a lie on top of it.
...I should've said it too. I didn't. That's on me.
lines.action: quiet, surprising herself

lines.text: We took the shortcut. I said yes to it. And when it mattered, I still tried to make it someone else's fault.
That was rough, honestly.
lines.action: quiet, not judgmental exactly, just true

lines.text: We took the shortcut. I let it get almost all the way to presentation day before I said anything. Better late than mid-collapse, I guess.
You still went to her without me. But I get why.
lines.action: grudging, arms loosening slightly

lines.text: We rebuilt the whole thing in one night — actually helped Mia catch up instead of just leaving her to it. That's why this feels earned instead of just correct.
We pulled an actual all-nighter for this. Feels different saying it out loud now.
lines.action: grinning, exhausted but proud

lines.text: We held the line on the shortcut, but I held the boundary hard — made Mia opt in on her own. It worked. It just cost some warmth to get there.
It's done. Late, rougher than I wanted. But it's ours.
lines.action: flat, tired, not quite looking at anyone

lines.text: I went around Mia first. That almost broke this. Talking it out after — actually talking, not just apologizing — is the only reason we're standing here like a group.
Next time, just tell me first. But — thanks. For fixing it right.
lines.action: genuinely warmer than she's let herself be all episode

lines.text: We talked, some. Not enough. I chose the simple version of the presentation, and I think some part of me chose the simple version of fixing this, too.
It's fine. We're fine.
lines.action: the kind of fine that isn't quite a lie and isn't quite true either

lines.text: I let Jordan carry the peace-making for days. It took until the literal last minute for me to actually say something real to Mia myself.
About time, honestly.
lines.action: relieved, stepping back to let them have the moment

lines.text: I let Jordan smooth it over instead of doing it myself. This is what that buys — clean, professional, and about six feet of distance that wasn't there before.
You two are gonna talk eventually, right?
lines.action: watching them both walk off in opposite directions

voiceNotes: Mia performs control through speed — she decides fast, states things as settled facts, and only softens when caught off guard (the 'don't tell him I said that' beat). Her cruelty is always wrapped in a laugh or a shrug, never delivered flat, which is what makes it deniable. Alex almost never argues out loud — his resistance is entirely physical (closing the sketchbook, going still, the pen tap). Any scene where he gets a full sentence out mid-excitement before catching himself is a small victory that should feel rare and specific. Jordan uses jokes as social lubricant and information-gathering simultaneously — 'okay honestly' is his tell that he's about to say something true. Wren's INTERNAL lines should do the emotional labor the character can't say out loud to the group — that's where her anxiety and dry humor actually live.

alternatives: Also, when I present, I'll just say the panel thing was mine — sounds better from me.
And obviously I'll take credit for the panel bit when I'm up there, it flows better.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Keeping it as an 'afterthought' delivery is important — Mia shouldn't announce this like a villain, it should sound like a logistics note to her, which is the actual horror of it.

alternatives: Must be nice. Must be really nice, having time to just do things twice.
Not all of us get to just redo our whole lives whenever, Wren.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Original line works well but this alt leans slightly more raw/insecure if the scene needs more vulnerability before her 'don't tell him' admission.
